mechanism: Passive flows are price-insensitive and mechanically allocate to the largest-cap names, reinforcing existing concentration. The inelastic markets hypothesis ($1 flow = $3-5 market cap) amplifies this, creating a Soros-ian reflexivity loop where concentration begets inflows begets more concentration.
regime_dependence: Strengthens as passive share increases (currently ~55%). Theoretical framework is sound but empirically untested — no passive-driven crash has occurred. Active managers may still set marginal prices.
